name: COMPOTE

ingredients.item: plus 1½ tbsp/200 ml heavy cream grated zest of 1 lemon
orange, rind shaved in long strips
plums (about 1 lb/500 g), pitted and cut into small cubes
cup/60 g whole wheat flour about 3 tbsp/30 g light brown sugar
tbsp/50 g unsalted butter, cut into small dice
cup/50 g blanched hazelnuts, lightly crushed
tbsp/20 g black sesame seeds coarse sea salt

method: Put the oil and orange rind in a small saucepan and place over medium heat.
When the orange starts to sizzle, remove it from the heat and leave the oil to cool with the orange rind immersed.

Put the plums, the remaining 2 tablespoons/25 g sugar, and the lemon juice in a saucepan and cook over medium-low heat for about 30 minutes, until it has a compote consistency, a bit runnier than jam. Set aside to cool.

To make the crumble, place the flour, sugar, butter, and ½ teaspoon coarse sea salt in a small bowl and, using your fingers, rub in the butter to form the consistency of bread crumbs. Mix in the hazelnuts and sesame seeds and spread out in a thin layer on a baking sheet. Bake for 15 minutes, until golden brown. Remove from the oven and set aside to cool before breaking the crumble up again with your fingers.

To assemble, spoon the cream mixture onto individual plates. Add a dollop of fruit on the side of the cream, followed by a scatter of the crumble. Drizzle ½ teaspoon of the orange-infused oil over each cheesecake and serve at once.
